title: disa draconis
intro: Disa draconis, also known as the dragon's mouth or fire lily, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to South Africa. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Disa draconis include treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, while also providing relief from arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Herbal preparations of Disa draconis can be made into infusions, decoctions, or ointments, and are often used to promote wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and support overall skin health.
